name: Signed Binary Proxy Execution - Network Detected
description: Detects the use of legitimate, signed Windows binaries like `certutil.exe` and `replace.exe` to make network connections. This is a common **Defense Evasion** technique where adversaries use trusted utilities to download malicious payloads or exfiltrate data, bypassing traditional security controls.

prompts.prompt: A critical alert has been triggered on the host **{host}**: a signed Windows binary, **{image}**, was detected making a network connection. The command line is **{command}**. This is a strong indicator of **Signed Binary Proxy Execution** (**MITRE ATT&CK T1218**), where an adversary is attempting to use a trusted utility to download a malicious payload or communicate with a command and control server. Immediate investigation is required to determine the legitimacy of this action and to remove the malicious file. The event occurred under the user **{user}**.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The name of the host where the event occurred.

parameters.name: image
parameters.description: The name of the signed binary that was executed.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: The full command line that was executed with the signed binary.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account that performed the action.
